sentences.text: In Sv39, Sv48, and Sv57, bits 62-61 of a leaf page table entry indicate the use of page-based memory types that override the PMA(s) for the associated memory pages.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The encoding for the PBMT bits is captured in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The Svpbmt extension depends on the Sv39 extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: .Encodings for PBMT field in Sv39, Sv48, and Sv57 PTEs. | |Mode |Value |Requested Memory Attributes |PMA + NC + IO + - |0 + 1 + 2 + 3 |None + Non-cacheable, idempotent, weakly-ordered (RVWMO), main memory + Non-cacheable, non-idempotent, strongly-ordered (I/O ordering), I/O + Reserved for future standard use |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Implementations may override additional PMAs not explicitly listed in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: For example, to be consistent with the characteristics of a typical I/O region, a misaligned memory access to a page with PBMT=IO might raise an exception, even if the underlying region were main memory and the same access would have succeeded for PBMT=PMA.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: For non-leaf PTEs, bits 62-61 are reserved for future standard use.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Until their use is defined by a standard extension, they must be cleared by software for forward compatibility, or else a page-fault exception is raised.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: For leaf PTEs, setting bits 62-61 to the value 3 is reserved for future standard use.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Until this value is defined by a standard extension, using this reserved value in a leaf PTE raises a page-fault exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: When PBMT settings override a main memory page into I/O or vice versa, memory accesses to such pages obey the memory ordering rules of the final effective attribute, as follows.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: If the underlying physical memory attribute for a page is I/O, and the page has PBMT=NC, then accesses to that page obey RVWMO.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: However, accesses to such pages are considered to be both I/O and main memory accesses for the purposes of FENCE, .aq, and .rl.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: If the underlying physical memory attribute for a page is main memory, and the page has PBMT=IO, then accesses to that page obey strong channel 0 I/O ordering rules.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: However, accesses to such pages are considered to be both I/O and main memory accesses for the purposes of FENCE, .aq, and .rl.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: When Svpbmt is used with non-zero PBMT encodings, it is possible for multiple virtual aliases of the same physical page to exist simultaneously with different memory attributes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: It is also possible for a U-mode or S-mode mapping through a PTE with Svpbmt enabled to observe different memory attributes for a given region of physical memory than a concurrent access to the same page performed by M-mode or when MODE=Bare.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: In such cases, the behaviors dictated by the attributes (including coherence, which is otherwise unaffected) may be violated.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Accessing the same location using different attributes that are both non-cacheable (e.g., NC and IO) does not cause loss of coherence, but might result in weaker memory ordering than the stricter attribute ordinarily guarantees.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Executing a `fence iorw, iorw` instruction between such accesses suffices to prevent loss of memory ordering.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Accessing the same location using different cacheability attributes may cause loss of coherence.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Executing the following sequence between such accesses prevents both loss of coherence and loss of memory ordering: `fence iorw, iorw`, followed by `cbo.flush` to an address of that location, followed by a `fence iorw, iorw`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: When two-stage address translation is enabled within the H extension, the page-based memory types are also applied in two stages.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: First, if `hgatp`.MODE is not equal to zero, non-zero G-stage PTE PBMT bits override the attributes in the PMA to produce an intermediate set of attributes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Otherwise, the PMAs serve as the intermediate attributes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Second, if `vsatp`.MODE is not equal to zero, non-zero VS-stage PTE PBMT bits override the intermediate attributes to produce the final set of attributes used by accesses to the page in question.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Otherwise, the intermediate attributes are used as the final set of attributes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: NOTE: These final attributes apply to implicit and explicit accesses that are subject to both stages of address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0

sentences.text: For accesses that are not subject to the first stage of address translation, e.g. VS-stage page-table accesses, the intermediate attributes apply instead.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Svpbmt" Extension for Page-Based Memory Types, Version 1.0
